steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Reading from the graph at $x = 0$, the function $y = f(x) + 4$ has a value of $y = 8$. The y-intercept is at the point $(0, 8)$.

steps.title: Find f(0) using the transformation
steps.content:
Since the graphed function is $y = f(x) + 4$, substitute the y-intercept point $(0, 8)$:
$$8 = f(0) + 4$$
Solve for $f(0)$:
$$f(0) = 4$$

steps.title: Use the exponential form to find the constant
steps.content:
All choices have the form $f(x) = -6^x + c$. Evaluate at $x = 0$:
$$f(0) = -6^0 + c = -1 + c$$
Since $f(0) = 4$, set up the equation:
$$-1 + c = 4$$
$$c = 5$$
Therefore $f(x) = -6^x + 5$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
